# RHDP Skills Marketplace

> AI-powered skills for Red Hat Demo Platform content creation and provisioning.
> Version: v2.14.0

## What this is

The RHDP Skills Marketplace provides Claude Code skills and agents for:
- Creating hands-on workshop content (Showroom AsciiDoc labs)
- Creating presenter-led demo content (Know/Show format)
- Validating content quality against Red Hat standards
- Building and validating AgnosticV catalog items
- Writing E2E automation (solve.yml + validate.yml via FTL)
- Generating documentation from skill definitions

## Architecture

Skills are orchestrators. Agents do the actual work in parallel.

Pattern: `skill (Sonnet) → spawns agents via Task tool → agents return JSON → skill merges + presents`

This is the same pattern as the FTL plugin (reference implementation).

## Skills

### showroom plugin

- `/showroom:create-lab` — Creates hands-on workshop modules. Spawns file-generator agents in parallel for new labs. Supports ph_payload headless mode for Publishing House.
- `/showroom:create-demo` — Creates presenter-led demo modules (Know/Show). Same agent pattern as create-lab.
- `/showroom:verify-content` — Validates content quality. Spawns scaffold-checker + N module-reviewers in parallel. ~6× faster than sequential. Supports ph_payload headless mode.
- `/showroom:blog-generate` — Transforms workshop/demo content to Markdown blog posts. Uses file-generator with FILE_TYPE=blog.

### agnosticv plugin

- `/agnosticv:catalog-builder` — Creates AgnosticV catalog items (common.yaml, dev.yaml, description.adoc). Calls workflow-reviewer agent after generation.
- `/agnosticv:validator` — Validates catalog configurations. Reads babylon.yaml schema as authoritative source. Checks multiuser htpasswd security, VS Code auth.

### ftl plugin

- `/ftl:rhdp-lab-validator` — Orchestrates 4 agents to write E2E automation: content-reader → solve-writer → validate-writer → env-connector.

### health plugin

- `/health:deployment-validator` — Validates that workloads deployed correctly. Live cluster checks.

## Agents

### showroom/agents/

- `showroom:scaffold-checker` (Haiku) — checks site.yml, ui-config.yml, antora.yml, gh-pages.yml
- `showroom:module-reviewer` (Sonnet) — reviews one .adoc module, B/C/D/E/F checks, returns dimension-scored JSON
- `showroom:file-generator` (Sonnet) — generates one AsciiDoc file (index|overview|details|module|blog|conclusion) from FULL_SPEC
- `showroom:score-aggregator` (Haiku) — aggregates dimension scores, detects regressions vs baseline
- `showroom:doc-writer` (Sonnet) — generates/updates GitHub Pages docs from SKILL.md files with Mermaid diagrams

### ftl/agents/

- `ftl:content-reader` (Sonnet) — reads .adoc, classifies steps
- `ftl:solve-writer` (Sonnet) — writes solve.yml from content-reader output
- `ftl:validate-writer` (Sonnet) — writes validate.yml playbooks
- `ftl:env-connector` (Sonnet) — pushes to live showroom, runs test cycle

### agnosticv/agents/

- `agnosticv:workflow-reviewer` (Sonnet) — checks builder/validator skill consistency

## Publishing House Integration

`verify-content` and `create-lab` / `create-demo` support headless mode via ph_payload JSON.

PH passes:
```yaml
ph_payload:
  content_path: content/modules/ROOT/pages/
  lab_type: workshop
  spec:
    lab_name: "..."
    learning_objectives: [...]
```

Skill runs all agents, returns structured JSON. Interactive UX unchanged. No PH code changes needed.

## External Source Priority

Skills and agents check external sources FIRST:
1. `$agv_path/.schemas/babylon.yaml` — authoritative schema for catalog validation
2. `showroom_template_nookbag` (e2e-template branch) — canonical buttons.js and E2E templates
3. Repo's own templates/prompts (`{REPO_PATH}/examples/`, `{REPO_PATH}/showroom/prompts/`)
4. Marketplace bundled files (`@showroom/templates/`, `@showroom/prompts/`)

## Personal Writing Style

All content creation skills accept a writing style profile:
- Describe in words: "conversational, short sentences, active voice"
- Paste example paragraphs
- Reference a file: `~/path/to/existing-module.adoc`
- Persistent profile: `~/.claude/context/my-writing-style.md`

Auto-humanizer runs on all generated prose — no AI writing patterns in output.

## Model Assignments

- Orchestrators (all skills): Sonnet 4.6
- Generation/review agents: Sonnet 4.6
- Reading/computation agents: Haiku 4.5

## Documentation

- GitHub Pages: https://rhpds.github.io/rhdp-skills-marketplace
- Agent architecture: /reference/agent-architecture
- PH integration: /reference/ph-integration
- Writing style: /reference/writing-style
